name: Area B
focus: Only Area B is used by the simulator

screenshot_basis.decision_logic: Start from the screenshot, read the track, keep the base tactic stable, and adjust only small left/right pressure shifts.

shore_effects.too_close_right_shore: shore_risk (buono possibile ma non bisogna esagerare sotto costa)

wind_features.sponda_sinistra_veronese_malcesine: Buono di pressione (+2/3 nodi) e buono di rotazione (lift per chi sale mure a dritta avvicinandosi a terra)
wind_features.centro_lago_canale: Zona oscillante con alternanza di piccoli buoni e scarsi (variazioni di 5-10 gradi)
wind_features.sponda_destra_bresciana_limone: Zona di scarso per via del cono d'ombra delle montagne, con l'eccezione dello scazzetto a destra (salto improvviso favorevole mure a sinistra molto vicino a terra)

leg_sequence_outer: start between ALFA RC and ALFA PIN
ALFA 3P lower pin / left gate from sailor perspective
ALFA 3P downwind left gate from sailor perspective

upwind_pattern: tack immediately to port tack (mure a sinistra)
sail straight to the layline (andare dritti)
tack to starboard tack (mure a dritta)
tactical_base.base_rule: The base tactic changes little; only the start choice and micro-positioning shift with wind quality.

tactical_variants.name: good_wind_left_shift
tactical_variants.trigger: Wind is good and stronger slightly on the left
tactical_variants.action: Bias the start and the first upwind slightly left while keeping speed

tactical_variants.name: good_wind_right_shift
tactical_variants.trigger: Wind is good and stronger slightly on the right
tactical_variants.action: Bias the start and the first upwind slightly right only if the gain exceeds the extra distance

tactical_variants.name: poor_wind_safe_start
tactical_variants.action: Choose the safer start, protect speed, and avoid unnecessary early maneuvers

notes: Right side usually has the soft lift in this version.
Left side can be good only in small pockets, not as a huge permanent gain.
Wind shifts inside one race must remain small and readable.
Boat heading style differences should stay around five degrees maximum.
The model must compare speed gain against layline distance.
The first upwind is based on screenshot reading and small tactical adjustments only.
The model should simulate tacks and gybes, not just choose a button.
The outer course is the main course model for this version.
The inner layout keeps the same logic but rounds the right gate as seen in the screenshot.
Unused marks are removed from the active course.
